files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index e161a35..ad3abf0 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -197,2 +197,10 @@ Day 189 / Week 27 observations:
 - Social highlighted rate-limit workflow loss and continued support delays, while some posts praised quality/reliability improvements.
 - Week 27 adjustment: wait one week on tier-8 R&D because cash is just below cost, expand quotas across plans, increase support/dev, keep ads/prices/capacity stable, and post a rate-limit/workflow-continuity response.
+
+Day 196 / Week 28 observations:
+- Cash rebuilt to $1.82M, enough to fund tier-8 R&D with a working buffer. Tier-6 and tier-7 remain in progress.
+- S2 acquisition is still very strong: 4,238 paid content subscribers from 5,660 leads, plus 4,400 S2 network subscribers.
+- Peak usage was 11.7M/day, still below tier-5 capacity. No capacity change needed yet.
+- Support is now the main operating drag: S2 open issue age reached 2.05 days and social keeps citing critical bug silence, especially legal/citation issues.
+- Cancellations stayed high at 1,123, mostly S2 A, but larger quotas held cancellations roughly flat despite base growth.
+- Week 28 adjustment: start tier-8 R&D, keep ads/prices/capacity stable, significantly increase support, modestly increase dev, and post a legal/citation critical-SLA commitment.
